Question: Verify that Stokes’ theorem is true for vector field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y , z ) = \langle y , x , \text{unicode[:-]} z \rangle$$ and surface S , where S is the upwardly oriented portion of the graph of $$f ( x , y ) = x^{2} y$$ over a triangle in the xy -plane with vertices $$( 0 , 0 ) ,$$ $$( 2 , 0 ) ,$$ and $$( 0 , 2 ) .$$

Use Stokes’ theorem to calculate surface integral $$\iint_{S} \text{curl} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp d \mathbf{\text{S}} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{F}} = \langle z , x , y \rangle$$ and S is the surface as shown in the following figure. The boundary curve, C , is oriented clockwise when looking along the positive y-axis.

Use Stokes’ theorem to calculate line integral $$\int_{C} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp d \mathbf{\text{r}} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{F}} = \langle z , x , y \rangle$$ and C is oriented clockwise and is the boundary of a triangle with vertices $$( 0 , 0 , 1 ) , ( 3 , 0 , -2 ) ,$$ and $$( 0 , 1 , 2 ) .$$
Calculate the curl of electric field E if the corresponding magnetic field is $$\mathbf{\text{B}} ( t ) = \langle t x , t y , -2 t z \rangle , 0 \le t < \infty .$$
